operating_state: Net sales reached $38.9 billion in Q3 2022, increasing 5.6% year-over-year driven by 4.3% comparable sales growth. Comparable average ticket rose 8.8% from inflation and new product demand while customer transactions declined 4.4% reflecting macroeconomic pressures. Online sales grew 9.6% to 13.3% of total sales supported by digital platform investments.
cost_structure: Gross profit margin compressed to 34.0% from 34.1% pressured by supply chain network investments and higher product and transportation costs, partially offset by retail price increases. SG&A expenses improved to 16.6% of sales from 16.8% benefiting from positive comparable sales leverage and lower incentive compensation while absorbing wage investments. Interest expense increased to 1.1% of sales from 0.9% due to higher debt balances and variable rate interest from swaps.
material_events: The company issued $7.0 billion in senior notes during the first nine months for general corporate purposes including debt repayment and share repurchases. Management announced a 15% quarterly dividend increase to $1.90 per share in February 2022. The Board approved a new $15.0 billion share repurchase authorization in August replacing the previous program.
macro_exposures: Currency headwinds from a stronger U.S. dollar negatively impacted net sales by $132 million in Q3 and $284 million year-to-date. The broader inflationary environment continues pressuring customer transaction volumes while driving average ticket increases. Global supply chain disruptions persist, prompting strategic inventory investments and merchandise pull-forward strategies.
forward_outlook: Management plans approximately $3 billion in capital expenditures for fiscal 2022, targeting roughly 2% of net sales annually with potential adjustments based on business operations or economic conditions. The company maintains disciplined capital allocation prioritizing business investment, dividend payments, and excess cash return through share repurchases while leveraging digital platform investments for interconnected retail strategy.
uncertainty_profile: Inventory turnover declined to 4.3 times from 5.4 times due to strategic inventory investments, supply chain facility investments, and spring seasonal carryover. The company maintains $14.0 billion remaining under share repurchase authorization with no expiration date. Working capital remains subject to seasonality, inventory management, vendor payment timing, and foreign exchange fluctuations.
